text: Setting clear goals at the start of a project is essential for success. One useful method is to set SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal might be "increase page load speed by 20% in three months." Once goals are defined, break them down into smaller milestones, like completing a design review. Each milestone helps track progress and leads to a final deliverable, such as a fully tested website. It is also important to ensure stakeholder alignment—everyone involved agrees on the goals and expected outcomes. Clear goals keep the team focused and reduce misunderstandings.
